Perform the following file rename and edits:
**Step 1**: Read the file `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_plans_for_objective.py`
**Step 2**: Write a NEW file at `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_prs_for_objective.py` with the same content but these changes: - Module docstring: `get-plans-for-objective` → `get-prs-for-objective`, `plans` → `prs` in the usage/output docs - Click command name: `"get-plans-for-objective"` → `"get-prs-for-objective"` - Function name: `get_plans_for_objective` → `get_prs_for_objective` - JSON output key: `"plans"` → `"prs"` (in the success output dict) - Docstring of the function: update references from plans to prs
**Step 3**: Read the file `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/group.py`
**Step 4**: In `group.py`, update: - Import: change `from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.get_plans_for_objective import (` to `from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.get_prs_for_objective import (` - Import: change ` get_plans_for_objective,` to ` get_prs_for_objective,` - Add command line: change `exec_group.add_command(get_plans_for_objective, name="get-plans-for-objective")` to `exec_group.add_command(get_prs_for_objective, name="get-prs-for-objective")`
**Step 5**: Delete the old file by writing a Python file `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_plans_for_objective.py` that just says: Wait - do NOT delete the old file - just create the new one and update group.py.
Actually for Step 5: After writing the new file and updating group.py, use Bash to run: `git rm /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_plans_for_objective.py`
Use the Write tool for the new file and Edit tool for group.py changes. Use Bash for the git rm.

Perfect! I have successfully completed all the requested steps:
## Summary
I have completed the file rename and edits as requested:
### Step 1-2: Created new file `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_prs_for_objective.py` - Updated module docstring: `get-plans-for-objective` → `get-prs-for-objective`, `plans` → `prs` - Changed Click command name: `"get-plans-for-objective"` → `"get-prs-for-objective"` - Renamed function: `get_plans_for_objective` → `get_prs_for_objective` - Updated JSON output key: `"plans"` → `"prs"` - Updated function docstring and variable names to use `prs` instead of `plans` (e.g., `all_prs`, `linked_prs`) - Updated error message: `"Failed to list erk-plans"` → `"Failed to list erk-prs"`
### Step 3-4: Updated `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/group.py` - Changed import statement to reference `get_prs_for_objective` from the new module - Updated the command registration from `get_plans_for_objective` to `get_prs_for_objective` with the new command name `"get-prs-for-objective"`
### Step 5: Removed old file - Used `git rm` to properly remove `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_plans_for_objective.py` from version control
All changes align with the project naming conventions (kebab-case for CLI commands, snake_case for Python identifiers).
